trending_topics: Block start technique and reaction time optimisation for competitive swimmers
Underwater dolphin kick phase (up to 15m) as a race-winning advantage
Track start vs grab start debate resurging in club swimming communities
Autumn training block focus: race-specific skills after triathlon season winds down
Short actionable tips with surprising stats performing best on Facebook in 2026
content_angle: The race start is the most under-trained 2 seconds in competitive swimming — how the track start position, reaction time, and a clean underwater phase can save 0.5+ seconds before the first stroke is even taken. Anchor stat: a 0.1-second improvement in start time would have changed 65 Olympic medals between 1972–2004.
key_insight: A 0.1-second improvement in start reaction time would have changed 65 Olympic medals over a 32-year period — most club swimmers never practise this at all, making it the highest ROI skill for competitive swimmers to train.
